summary: Iteration 30: Full validation of advanced pagination (<<, <, page-input + Go, >, >>, page-size) on /interview-reports + /update-scores AND DB-level MESSAGING_CUTOFF_TS guard on bg_workers. Backend 9/9 pytest PASS in 2m: pagination edge cases (last page, beyond-last empty 200, response<10s), distinct rows across pages, classification regression (100798/15555 unchanged), applicants pagination, plus negative+positive cutoff DB tests. Cutoff log present on startup. Frontend: All 8 testids present on both pages; disable rules correct (first/prev disabled on page 1; next/last disabled on page 50); Go to N + invalid input revert + page-size reset-to-1 verified; /update-scores modal Save preserves page 2. ALL FUNCTIONAL FLOWS PASS.

issues: The Emergent preview floating badge ('Made with Emergent') in the bottom-right corner visually overlays/intercepts the last-page-btn (>>) and go-page-btn in this preview environment. Functional code is correct — JS-level clicks succeed. Will not affect production deployment without the badge. Consider extra bottom padding (mb-12) on the page wrapper or moving pagination higher to avoid overlap in preview.

backend_pagination: GET /api/bb/interview-reports?page=N+1 returns 200 with empty data array
GET /api/bb/attended-for-scores contract preserved (data,total,page,limit,totalPages); pages 1 vs 2 distinct emails
messaging_cutoff: Backend startup log confirmed: '[CutoffGuard] MESSAGING_CUTOFF_TS=2026-05-04T18:07:51.691447+00:00 — only post-cutoff registrations will be messaged'
Negative case: inserted bb_registrations doc with registered_at=2020-01-01, schedule_time=now+2h, is_shortlisted=true — after 70s otp_sent remained False, no OTP set. Doc deleted.

Positive case: inserted doc with registered_at=now, otp_sent=True observed within ~30-60s; otp/otpGeneratedAt/otpExpiry/otp_sent_at all set. TEST_MODE re-routed phone to 9443109903 and email to rishi.nayak@blubridge.com (verified in backend.err.log: '[TEST_MODE] Overriding recipient'). Doc deleted.
frontend_interview_reports: All 8 testids present: first-page-btn, prev-page-btn, page-input, go-page-btn, next-page-btn, last-page-btn, page-size-select, pagination
On page 1: first-page-btn and prev-page-btn have disabled attribute = true
Click last-page-btn -> network GET ...?page=50&limit=100 -> label 'Page 50 of 50 (4919 records)'
Type 5 + click go-page-btn -> network GET ...?page=5&limit=100 -> 'Page 5 of 50'
Type 99999 + Go -> remains on Page 5 (input reverts)
frontend_update_scores: Click first 'Update' button on page 2, modal opens with prefilled data, click Save -> 'Updated' toast appears AND label still 'Page 2 of 11' (no jump to page 1). In-place update verified.

action_items: OPTIONAL UX: increase bottom padding on /interview-reports and /update-scores so the Emergent preview badge does not visually overlap last-page-btn. Not a functional bug — production won't have the badge.
critical_code_review_comments: Pagination.jsx: handleGo correctly reverts on invalid input but does NOT reset goInput when user types and presses Go with current page (no-op acceptable).

InterviewReports.js line 48: useEffect re-runs fetchData(1, pageSize) on every fetchData identity change (filter changes), which is intentional. navPage(pg) sets page state AND calls fetchData(pg) — both update sources are consistent. Safe.
UpdateScores.js: modal Save handler correctly re-fetches CURRENT page (page-2-preserved verified). Good.

bg_workers.py _cutoff_filter() applied to all 5 workers; default '9999-12-31' fail-safe is correct (sends nothing if env missing). bb_modules.py also gates synchronous schedule-confirmation path. Coverage complete.

Minor: Pagination component `if (!total || total <= 0) return null` hides controls when there's truly zero data — fine for pagination, but a 'No records' state still renders elsewhere in the page. No issue.

success_rate.backend: 100% (9/9 pytest passed: 7 pagination/regression in 12s + 2 cutoff DB tests in 106s)
success_rate.frontend: 100% functional (all flows pass via JS click; preview-only badge overlay noted)

seed_data_creation: Two transient bb_registrations docs (isTest=true, TEST_iter30_neg* and TEST_iter30_pos*) inserted then deleted by tests in their try/finally. No leftover test data. UpdateScores modal Save mutated one real applicant on page 2 of /update-scores (status field) — matches existing iter29 pattern.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration30_cutoff_pagination.py is a regression suite (auth: Admin User/Admin User). The TestMessagingCutoff class takes ~2 minutes (waits for 30s OTP-worker cycles) — run separately or with -k 'not MessagingCutoff' for a fast check. Note: the Emergent preview floating badge in bottom-right corner intercepts pointer events on right-edge pagination buttons; use `page.evaluate("el.click()")` instead of `page.click(force=True)` for reliable Playwright tests. MESSAGING_CUTOFF_TS=2026-05-04T18:07:51.691447+00:00 in /app/backend/.env. TEST_MODE=true reroutes to rishi.nayak@blubridge.com / 9443109903.

mocked_apis.WhatsApp AiSensy: Returns 401 — gracefully handled, not exercised in iter30 cutoff tests
